title: Render Tree
objectives: Know where painting and hit-testing happen

sections.markdown: The RenderObject tree does the actual work: layout (measuring and positioning), painting, and hit-testing. Most use RenderBox, which works in a 2D Cartesian box model.

Flutter's layout rule: **constraints go down, sizes go up, parent sets position**. A parent passes constraints (min/max width/height) to a child; the child picks its size within them and returns it; the parent positions it. This single-pass layout is why Flutter is O(n) and fast.

sections.code:
Parent --constraints--> Child
Child  --size-------->  Parent
Parent --sets offset--> Child position
(one pass, no multi-pass reflow like the web)

sections.markdown: Unbounded constraints crashes: putting a Column (wants infinite height) inside another scrollable, or a ListView with no height. Fix with Expanded/Flexible, shrinkWrap, or a SizedBox with bounds.
RepaintBoundary isolates a subtree's painting so animating one part doesn't repaint the whole screen — a key performance tool for complex/animated UIs.

flashcards.front: What does the RenderObject tree do?

flashcards.back: Constraints go down, sizes go up, parent sets position — a single pass.
Isolates a subtree so its repaints don't force the rest of the screen to repaint.

quiz.q: Which tree handles layout and painting?
quiz.explain: RenderObjects measure, position, paint and hit-test.

options: Constraints down, sizes up, parent positions
quiz.explain: Parents pass constraints; children return sizes; parents set offsets — one pass.
It limits repaint to the bounded subtree.

challenges.text: Trigger and fix an 'unbounded height' error in a Column/ListView.
Use Expanded/Flexible to satisfy constraints in a Row.
Wrap an animating widget in a RepaintBoundary and confirm reduced repaints.
Write a custom RenderObject (extends RenderBox) implementing performLayout and paint.

interviewQuestions: What is the RenderObject tree responsible for?
What causes unbounded-constraint errors and how to fix them?
What is RepaintBoundary and when use it?
